{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant, Warehouse",
  "description": "International Medical Corps is a global, humanitarian, nonprofit organization dedicated to saving lives and relieving suffering through health care training and relief and development programs. Established in 1984 by volunteer doctors and Nurses, International Medical Corps is a private, voluntary, nonpolitical, nonsectarian organization. Its mission is to improve the quality of life through health interventions and related activities that build local capacity in under-served communities worldwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant, Warehouse

Job Location: Katsina

Job Summary


	The Assistant, Warehouse is assisting supervisor in accounting for goods stored in the warehouse, their proper management under good storage conditions and for documenting and reporting all warehouse transactions in a transparent manner and in accordance and compliance with International Medical Corps Warehouse Manual, donor requirements and local laws, as applicable.
	To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential function with or without reasonable accommodation:


Main Tasks and Responsibilties
Assistant, Warehouse is assisting supervisor in:&nbsp;


	Implementation of IMC warehouse procedures as per IMC Warehouse Manual&nbsp;
	Ensuring appropriate storage condition for all commodities, including temperature and humidity control, and isolation of dangerous goods and goods not fit for end use (i.e., damaged/expired goods).&nbsp;
	Monitoring stored items shelf life
	Reporting of any losses or damages of commodities, processing stock adjustments and alerting stock owners on discrepancies that should be investigated.&nbsp;
	Maintaining accurate stock records by item and project.&nbsp;
	Maintenance of relevant handling equipment and availability of packing materials, cleaning materials, and protective equipment and supplies
	Supervising casual labor working in the warehouse, as well as visitors. &nbsp;
	Reception of goods physical count (preparation of GRN) and Quantity Control processing with management assigned staff.&nbsp;
	Ensuring that the proper stacking and organizing of stocks and optimizing the use of warehouse indoor/outdoor space.&nbsp;
	Quality control over kitting/de-kitting/reconditioning/reconstituting operations.&nbsp;
	Preparing release of goods in line with approved Goods Issue Orders (GIOs). &nbsp;
	Ensuring that the items in the warehouse and outgoing dispatches are appropriately packed and thus reducing the risk of damage during handling and transportation.&nbsp;
	Overseeing loading of commodities and documenting cargo handover to transporter/recipient.&nbsp;
	Ensuring warehouse cleaning, disinfecting and making sure it is kept dry, well-organized and pest/rodents free, etc.&nbsp;


Compliance, Audit and related controls:


	Responsible for compliance of performed or supervised warehouse activities in line with IMC policies and procedures and applicable donor rules s and local laws and regulation.&nbsp;
	Assists in timely submission or availability of mandatory reports to the country management team, in line with IMC policies and procedures.&nbsp;
	Implementing of correctives measures identified as result audit or compliance review.&nbsp;


Fraud prevention:


	Committed to fraud prevention, detection, reporting and fraud risk reduction as per IMC policies and procedures, donor rules and local laws and regulations.&nbsp;


Logistics Management &amp; Information System (LMIS):


	Use software solutions in a consistent and accurate manner.
	Perform other duties as assigned. The duties and responsibilities listed in this document are representative of the nature and level of work assigned and not necessarily all inclusive.


Minimum Qualifications


	Typically, a Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain &nbsp;or related area. Equivalent combination of relevant education and experience may be substituted as appropriate.
	Typically, 6 months experience in Warehouse and/or Logistics
	Good interpersonal and team skills.&nbsp;
	Commitment to and understanding of IMC&rsquo;s aims, values and principles.&nbsp;
	Basic knowledge of MS Office including MS Word, Excel and Outlook.&nbsp;
	Ability to integrate and work well within multi-ethnic and multicultural team. &nbsp;
	Ability to work in harsh conditions, often in remote areas, &nbsp;
	Relevant qualifications or training courses.&nbsp;
	Fluency in written and spoken English.&nbsp;


Additional Technical or Language Requirements:


	Must have a valid Pharmacist license - because he / she will be managing medical warehouse.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-30",

Assistant, Warehouse Job at International Medical Corps




        
    

        
        International Medical Corps is a global, humanitarian, nonprofit organization dedicated to saving lives and relieving suffering through health care training and relief and development programs. Established in 1984 by volunteer doctors and Nurses, International Medical Corps is a private, voluntary, nonpolitical, nonsectarian organization. Its mission is to improve the quality of life through health interventions and related activities that build local capacity in under-served communities worldwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant, Warehouse

Job Location: Katsina

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Assistant, Warehouse is assisting supervisor in accounting for goods stored in the warehouse, their proper management under good storage conditions and for documenting and reporting all warehouse transactions in a transparent manner and in accordance and compliance with International Medical Corps Warehouse Manual, donor requirements and local laws, as applicable.
	To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential function with or without reasonable accommodation:


Main Tasks and Responsibilties
Assistant, Warehouse is assisting supervisor in: 


	Implementation of IMC warehouse procedures as per IMC Warehouse Manual 
	Ensuring appropriate storage condition for all commodities, including temperature and humidity control, and isolation of dangerous goods and goods not fit for end use (i.e., damaged/expired goods). 
	Monitoring stored items shelf life
	Reporting of any losses or damages of commodities, processing stock adjustments and alerting stock owners on discrepancies that should be investigated. 
	Maintaining accurate stock records by item and project. 
	Maintenance of relevant handling equipment and availability of packing materials, cleaning materials, and protective equipment and supplies
	Supervising casual labor working in the warehouse, as well as visitors.  
	Reception of goods physical count (preparation of GRN) and Quantity Control processing with management assigned staff. 
	Ensuring that the proper stacking and organizing of stocks and optimizing the use of warehouse indoor/outdoor space. 
	Quality control over kitting/de-kitting/reconditioning/reconstituting operations. 
	Preparing release of goods in line with approved Goods Issue Orders (GIOs).  
	Ensuring that the items in the warehouse and outgoing dispatches are appropriately packed and thus reducing the risk of damage during handling and transportation. 
	Overseeing loading of commodities and documenting cargo handover to transporter/recipient. 
	Ensuring warehouse cleaning, disinfecting and making sure it is kept dry, well-organized and pest/rodents free, etc. 


Compliance, Audit and related controls:


	Responsible for compliance of performed or supervised warehouse activities in line with IMC policies and procedures and applicable donor rules s and local laws and regulation. 
	Assists in timely submission or availability of mandatory reports to the country management team, in line with IMC policies and procedures. 
	Implementing of correctives measures identified as result audit or compliance review. 


Fraud prevention:


	Committed to fraud prevention, detection, reporting and fraud risk reduction as per IMC policies and procedures, donor rules and local laws and regulations. 


Logistics Management & Information System (LMIS):


	Use software solutions in a consistent and accurate manner.
	Perform other duties as assigned. The duties and responsibilities listed in this document are representative of the nature and level of work assigned and not necessarily all inclusive.


Minimum Qualifications


	Typically, a Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain  or related area. Equivalent combination of relevant education and experience may be substituted as appropriate.
	Typically, 6 months experience in Warehouse and/or Logistics
	Good interpersonal and team skills. 
	Commitment to and understanding of IMC’s aims, values and principles. 
	Basic knowledge of MS Office including MS Word, Excel and Outlook. 
	Ability to integrate and work well within multi-ethnic and multicultural team.  
	Ability to work in harsh conditions, often in remote areas,  
	Relevant qualifications or training courses. 
	Fluency in written and spoken English. 


Additional Technical or Language Requirements:


	Must have a valid Pharmacist license - because he / she will be managing medical warehouse.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Devien Consult Limited&nbsp; - Our client, an HR consulting firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:


Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary&nbsp;


	We are seeking a smart, organized, and proactive Personal Assistant to provide administrative and operational support to a busy executive.


Job Requirements


	1&ndash;2 years of experience in a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, Administrative, or similar role
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Professional appearance and demeanor.




Salary
N100,000 &ndash; N150,000 Monthly.



Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Personal Assistant Job at Devien Consult Limited




        
    

        
        Devien Consult Limited  - Our client, an HR consulting firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:


Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a smart, organized, and proactive Personal Assistant to provide administrative and operational support to a busy executive.


Job Requirements


	1–2 years of experience in a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, Administrative, or similar role
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Professional appearance and demeanor.




Salary
N100,000 – N150,000 Monthly.



Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

	We are currently recruiting for a smart, organised, and detail-oriented Back-End Operations Coordinator for a premium hospitality and creative space in Lagos.

Back-end Pperations Coordinator Job at ClearView Professional Consults Limited




        
    

        
        ClearView Professional Consults Limited is a consulting and business support firm dedicated to helping businesses and individuals stay financially organised, compliant, and structured for growth. We understand that running a business means wearing many hats. From keeping proper records to managing tax, payroll, people, and day-to-day operations, the workload can get messy fast. That is where we come in. Whether you are a startup finding your footing, a small business looking to scale, or an established company trying to tighten operations, we bring practical support that keeps things moving properly.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Back-end Pperations Coordinator
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Junior–Mid Level Role)

Job Description


	We are currently recruiting for a smart, organised, and detail-oriented Back-End Operations Coordinator for a premium hospitality and creative space in Lagos.
	This role is ideal for someone who thrives on structure, organisation, and operational excellence, and enjoys ensuring things run smoothly behind the scenes.


This role is ideal for someone who:


	Is highly organised and detail-oriented
	Enjoys structure, systems, and operational coordination
	Can manage inventory, housekeeping, and operations efficiently
	Is tech-savvy and comfortable learning new systems
	Can work independently and maintain accountability
	Is eager to grow into higher operational responsibilities


Key Responsibilities


	Operations oversight & daily coordination
	Inventory & stock management
	Housekeeping coordination & quality control
	Maintenance tracking & vendor coordination
	Documentation & operational reporting
	Procurement coordination
	SOP & workflow management
	Internal operations coordination


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2-3 years of experience.
	Strong organisational and administrative skills
	Good communication and coordination skills
	Microsoft Office / Google Workspace proficiency
	Ability to manage multiple operational tasks effectively
	Hospitality or operations experience is an advantage
	Familiarity with systems/software (SageOne or similar is a plus)
	Smart, proactive, and willing to learn.


Salary and Benefits


	N200,000 / Month.
	Transportation support for late shifts
	Overtime compensation
	Staff meals provided (3 times weekly)
	Career growth opportunity into senior operational roles
	Paid leave after one year of employment

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager",
  "description": "Travicon Global Services Limited is a professional HR consultancy firm providing recruitment, talent management, and workforce solutions to organizations within Nigeria and internationally. We also offer expatriate support services, including visa processing, immigration assistance, relocation, and settlement services, helping individuals and businesses navigate cross-border employment and mobility with ease.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Lugbe, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Business Development Manager on behalf of Fabric Butik to drive business growth, expand market presence, and develop strategic partnerships within the textile and fashion industry.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new business opportunities in local and international markets.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, distributors, retailers, manufacturers, and corporate clients.
	Develop and implement sales and marketing strategies to increase revenue and market share.
	Conduct market research to identify industry trends, customer needs, and competitor activities.
	Generate leads, prepare proposals, negotiate contracts, and close sales deals.
	Meet and exceed sales targets and business growth objectives.
	Coordinate with production, procurement, and logistics teams to ensure timely delivery of products.
	Attend trade fairs, exhibitions, and industry events to promote the company&rsquo;s textile products and services.
	Monitor customer satisfaction and resolve complaints to maintain long-term business relationships.
	Prepare periodic sales forecasts, business development reports, and market analysis reports.
	Identify opportunities for product diversification and market expansion.
	Collaborate with management to develop pricing strategies and promotional campaigns.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Textile Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of two years of proven experience in business development, sales, or marketing, preferably in the textile or manufacturing industry.
	Strong negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.
	Good understanding of textile products, production processes, and market trends.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM software.
	Ability to work independently and achieve sales targets.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: evshnigeria@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Manager Job at Travicon Global Services Limited




        
    

        
        Travicon Global Services Limited is a professional HR consultancy firm providing recruitment, talent management, and workforce solutions to organizations within Nigeria and internationally. We also offer expatriate support services, including visa processing, immigration assistance, relocation, and settlement services, helping individuals and businesses navigate cross-border employment and mobility with ease.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Lugbe, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Business Development Manager on behalf of Fabric Butik to drive business growth, expand market presence, and develop strategic partnerships within the textile and fashion industry.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new business opportunities in local and international markets.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, distributors, retailers, manufacturers, and corporate clients.
	Develop and implement sales and marketing strategies to increase revenue and market share.
	Conduct market research to identify industry trends, customer needs, and competitor activities.
	Generate leads, prepare proposals, negotiate contracts, and close sales deals.
	Meet and exceed sales targets and business growth objectives.
	Coordinate with production, procurement, and logistics teams to ensure timely delivery of products.
	Attend trade fairs, exhibitions, and industry events to promote the company’s textile products and services.
	Monitor customer satisfaction and resolve complaints to maintain long-term business relationships.
	Prepare periodic sales forecasts, business development reports, and market analysis reports.
	Identify opportunities for product diversification and market expansion.
	Collaborate with management to develop pricing strategies and promotional campaigns.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Textile Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of two years of proven experience in business development, sales, or marketing, preferably in the textile or manufacturing industry.
	Strong negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.
	Good understanding of textile products, production processes, and market trends.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM software.
	Ability to work independently and achieve sales targets.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "title": "Finance and Accounts Lead",
  "description": "Ren San &amp; Co. is a business support and full-service brand strategy firm. Our purpose is to help clients achieve their goals by developing and growing their brand, project, or event. We think of ourselves as more than just service providers, we are an extension of their team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance and Accounts Lead
Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Finance &amp; Accounts Lead will take ownership of the financial operations across our multiple business entities.
	This is a role that demands strong cashflow control, rigorous financial discipline, accurate and timely reporting, and the ability to translate complex financial data into clear insights for leadership.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Operations &amp; Accounting:


	Manage day-to-day accounting functions including accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, and bank reconciliations.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records for all business units.
	Process invoices, payments, and expense claims in line with company policies.


Cashflow &amp; Treasury Management:


	Track, monitor, and forecast cashflow across multiple entities to ensure sufficient liquidity for operations.
	Prepare regular cashflow statements and flag potential shortfalls proactively.
	Recommend and implement strategies to optimise working capital.


Financial Reporting &amp; Analysis:


	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports (income statement, balance sheet, cashflow statement) with variance analysis.
	Develop annual budgets and rolling forecasts in collaboration with business heads.
	Provide leadership with clear, simplified commentaries on financial performance and trends.


Cost Control &amp; Expense Management:


	Oversee expense management systems, ensuring all expenditures are properly authorised, documented, and within budget.
	Identify cost-saving opportunities and enforce cost discipline across the group.
	Review and refine internal controls to prevent waste, fraud, and errors.


Audit, Tax &amp; Compliance:


	Coordinate all external audit engagements, ensuring timely and clean audit outcomes.
	Liaise with tax consultants and regulatory bodies to ensure full compliance with FIRS, state, and local tax requirements (VAT, WHT, CIT, PAYE, etc.).
	Prepare and file all statutory returns accurately and on time.
	Stay updated on changes in financial regulations and accounting standards (IFRS).


Stakeholder &amp; Advisor Management:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for auditors, tax advisors, bankers, and other financial partners.
	Support leadership with financial insights for strategic decision-making, pricing, and business planning.


Qualifications
The ideal candidate should possess:


	5-10 years of working experience in insurance brokerage, insurance sales, or business development.
	Strong professional network within insurance companies, underwriting firms, and corporate organisations.
	Proven track record of premium generation and revenue growth.
	Excellent understanding of insurance products, risk management, and brokerage operations.
	Demonstrated ability to lead business development initiatives successfully.


Skills &amp; Requirements:


	Strong sales, negotiation, and closing skills.
	Deep knowledge of insurance products and market operations.
	Commercial and revenue-focused mindset.
	Excellent relationship management and stakeholder engagement skills.
	Strong communication and presentation abilities.
	Strategic thinking and analytical problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently and lead high-performing teams.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM tools is an added advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: ryanrensan@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Finance and Accounts Lead Job at Ren San & Co




        
    

        
        Ren San & Co. is a business support and full-service brand strategy firm. Our purpose is to help clients achieve their goals by developing and growing their brand, project, or event. We think of ourselves as more than just service providers, we are an extension of their team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance and Accounts Lead
Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description

	The Finance & Accounts Lead will take ownership of the financial operations across our multiple business entities.
	This is a role that demands strong cashflow control, rigorous financial discipline, accurate and timely reporting, and the ability to translate complex financial data into clear insights for leadership.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Operations & Accounting:


	Manage day-to-day accounting functions including accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, and bank reconciliations.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records for all business units.
	Process invoices, payments, and expense claims in line with company policies.


Cashflow & Treasury Management:


	Track, monitor, and forecast cashflow across multiple entities to ensure sufficient liquidity for operations.
	Prepare regular cashflow statements and flag potential shortfalls proactively.
	Recommend and implement strategies to optimise working capital.


Financial Reporting & Analysis:


	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports (income statement, balance sheet, cashflow statement) with variance analysis.
	Develop annual budgets and rolling forecasts in collaboration with business heads.
	Provide leadership with clear, simplified commentaries on financial performance and trends.


Cost Control & Expense Management:


	Oversee expense management systems, ensuring all expenditures are properly authorised, documented, and within budget.
	Identify cost-saving opportunities and enforce cost discipline across the group.
	Review and refine internal controls to prevent waste, fraud, and errors.


Audit, Tax & Compliance:


	Coordinate all external audit engagements, ensuring timely and clean audit outcomes.
	Liaise with tax consultants and regulatory bodies to ensure full compliance with FIRS, state, and local tax requirements (VAT, WHT, CIT, PAYE, etc.).
	Prepare and file all statutory returns accurately and on time.
	Stay updated on changes in financial regulations and accounting standards (IFRS).


Stakeholder & Advisor Management:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for auditors, tax advisors, bankers, and other financial partners.
	Support leadership with financial insights for strategic decision-making, pricing, and business planning.


Qualifications
The ideal candidate should possess:


	5-10 years of working experience in insurance brokerage, insurance sales, or business development.
	Strong professional network within insurance companies, underwriting firms, and corporate organisations.
	Proven track record of premium generation and revenue growth.
	Excellent understanding of insurance products, risk management, and brokerage operations.
	Demonstrated ability to lead business development initiatives successfully.


Skills & Requirements:


	Strong sales, negotiation, and closing skills.

	Strong communication and presentation abilities.
